stem: Should coal mining be completely stopped in forest areas? Arg I: No, coal is essential for energy security. Arg II: Yes, mining destroys forest ecosystems.

stem: Should India invest more in underground coal gasification? Arg I: Yes, it will unlock deep coal reserves. Arg II: No, it requires advanced technology.

stem: Should the retirement age of mining engineers be raised to 65? Arg I: Yes, experienced engineers are valuable. Arg II: No, younger engineers need opportunities.

stem: Should CIL diversify into renewable energy? Arg I: Yes, it will future-proof the company. Arg II: No, it should focus on its core coal business.

stem: Should mine safety inspections be conducted without prior notice? Arg I: Yes, it catches real compliance status. Arg II: No, it may disrupt operations.

stem: Should the government provide subsidies for coal washeries? Arg I: Yes, cleaner coal reduces pollution. Arg II: No, washeries should be economically viable.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should India increase coking coal imports to boost steel production? Arg I: Yes, domestic coking coal is limited. Arg II: No, it affects the trade deficit.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should drone surveillance be mandatory for all opencast mines? Arg I: Yes, it improves safety monitoring. Arg II: No, it increases operational costs.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should there be a single national mining regulatory authority? Arg I: Yes, it ensures uniform standards. Arg II: No, states have different mining contexts.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should the MMDR Act be further amended to allow private mining? Arg I: Yes, it will increase competition. Arg II: No, public sector is more accountable.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should first aid training be mandatory for all mine workers? Arg I: Yes, it saves lives in emergencies. Arg II: No, it takes time away from production.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should mine closure plans be approved before mining begins? Arg I: Yes, ensures land reclamation commitment. Arg II: No, conditions may change over decades.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should CIL use more contract labour in underground mines? Arg I: Yes, it reduces fixed costs. Arg II: No, it compromises safety accountability.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
stem: Should mining companies be mandated to spend 5% of profit on local community development? Arg I: Yes, mining affects local communities. Arg II: No, it reduces profitability.

subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
